hints.text: **Shorter-than-normal** white plates + EU strip
Outer lines **always solid white** (SE/NO use dashes — exception: Åland!); middle lines: white dashes ± solid white, solid yellow + white dashes, or double yellow (yellow being phased out)

shared_with.note: Sweden never has yellow middles; outers dashed on small roads.
Norwegian middles have an orange tint + dashed outers.

hints.text: Long b&w bollards: **rectangular white front reflector + two dots on the back**, diagonal black strip (round or thin-curved)
Snow poles orange w/ **thin white band near the TOP**
**Finnish**: unrelated to most of Europe — long words, double vowels, ä/ö

shared_with.note: Estonian is closely related and looks similar.

hints.text: Street names end **-tie / -katu** (also -polku, -kuja); street signs white w/ black text
Road signs **yellow background** + red-border signs w/ a **thin yellow stroke on the edge** — no other Nordic country has that stroke
hints.uniq_note: Sweden/Iceland also have yellow signs — the thin yellow edge stroke is the Finland-only detail.

hints.text: Pedestrian signs **5 stripes** (SE/NO usually 4); directional signs solid blue w/ white border-arrows; chevrons **black w/ yellow arrows** (NO/IS similar; SE = blue-yellow)
Wooden poles; lamps mounted **at the top w/ straight arm + double bolting** (SE: lower + curved); **blue-white striped signposts** (SE too; Estonian ones wider/looser)
Dense **boreal forest** (pines + white-trunk birches); Gen3 often in **autumn** (rare in other Nordics — fall vibe = Finland tell); **lakes everywhere** ('Land of a Thousand Lakes')
Modern utilitarian architecture; wooden cladding; **Falu-red** country houses (like SE/NO)
Only **two bus-stop styles**: blue-white or yellow-black (Sweden has many, mostly with stop names)
**K-Market & S-Market** supermarkets exist only in Finland
**Shitcam** coverage (most in Europe together w/ Spain); Gen4 smallcam
**Regional 3–4-digit road numbers: first digit = region**, 1 (south) → 9 (north); 2-digit majors don't follow

**Region metas**: Lapland big roads (2023+) = snow poles w/ 2 long reflectors; farmland → west + south coast; wind turbines → west coast; stunted spindly trees → north; **Gen3 snow only in far-NW Lapland**
**Swedish bilingual** → south + west coasts (Swedish inland ≈ never); **Sámi bilingual** (uses the letter **c**) → far north
Gen3 antenna metas: slightly **tilted long antenna** → southwest; **no antenna** → south-central

**Åland**: monolingual Swedish; red-tinted pavement + **dashed outer lines** (Sweden-like); short fully-white plates w/ blue text, no EU strip; some Swedish 4-stripe ped signs; bus stops w/ white 4-digit plate (+semicircle sign); **no Gen4**
**Road 970** (Norwegian border): river valley, river usually NW, stunted birches, dead autumn Gen3; **Kilpisjärvi** = hilly low-vegetation Scandes foothills (spring Gen2, dead look)

**Helsinki**: much Gen4 sidewalk trekker downtown; **green-yellow trams** (Tampere's are red); buses blue-white w/ HSL logo; **Tampere** buses 'NYSSE'; Turku = yellow buses w/ white rear; Jyväskylä = green buses; Oulu = pink-front buses

**Tampere**: red metal plates w/ 7 golden protrusions (houses/lamps/posts); Seinäjoki & Isokyrö = unique blue street signs; **Lahti** = black-border ALL-CAPS street signs (Kuopio thicker font); Kerava = blue-yellow coat of arms top-left; Järvenpää = blue-grey arms + neighbourhood above the name
